Motion To Compel Subpoena For Business Records For Gonzales Home
Off Calendar Aug-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs TERESA WONG, VAN NESS CARE CENTER, INC., SAN FRANCISCO CARE CENTER, L.P., and MEL LEE MANAGEMENT, INC. Motion To Compel Subpoena For Business Records For Gonzales Home

Defendants' motion to compel production of third party business records is unopposed and granted. The court has balanced the third party's right of privacy with defendant's need for the information and finds defendants have demonstrated relevancy of the information related to their defense outweighs third party privacy concerns. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 143 Cal. Rpt. 695. The Protective Order now in place protects the third party's right of privacy and a copy shall be provided forthwith to the third party. A copy of this Order with Notice of Entry shall be served on the third party. The Order shall require confidential production of the documents covered by the subpoena within thirty days of service The defendant shall meet and confer with the third-party for the purpose of logistics relating to delivery of responsive documentation. The information sought may then be extracted in summary form. If documents identifying occupancy rates, vacancy rates, and racial demographic of facility residents for the period covered by the subpoena do not exist the third party may represent same in a verified pleading. If documents responsive to said categories are withheld based on privilege a privilege log shall be served.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling via email to sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Joint Motion And Joint Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Verified Answer
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jason Fellner, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, T:415.788.1900, for defendants/moving party;
David Millstein, Esq. of Millstein & Associates, for defendants/moving party;
Megan E. Ryan, Esq. Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3970, for plantiff/opposing party.

RULING: Defendants contested only the conditions portion of the tentative ruling. The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS TERESA WONG, VAN NESS CARE CENTER, INC., SAN FRANCISCO CARE CENTER, L.P., MEL LEE MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Joint Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Verified Answer in its entirety, as follows

Defendants' joint motion for leave of Court to file second amended verified answer is granted on the conditions that Defendants re-open the individual depositions of Melvin Lee and Teresa Wong related to the new affirmative defenses in the second amended verified answer and serve substantive responses to Plaintiffs' discovery requests related to the new affirmative defenses within 30 days of service of such requests. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion avoid any legal prejudice to Plaintiffs by the granting of the motion. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(a)(1), 576 [court is authorized to grant leave "on such terms as may be proper"].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Defendants' joint motion for leave of Court to file cross-complaint is granted on the conditions that Defendants re-open the individual depositions of Melvin Lee and Teresa Wong related to any new allegations in the cross-complaint and serve substantive responses to the City's discovery requests related to the allegations in the cross-complaint within 30 days of service of such requests.
